essence: Optimises CNC machining processes for customers, providing technical expertise, training, and advanced application support.

identity.title: CNC Applications Engineer
identity.summary: Works with customers to improve machining performance, develop optimised toolpaths, recommend tooling strategies, and ensure CNC systems deliver maximum productivity and precision.

salary.notes: Applications engineers in aerospace, robotics, or OEM‑level roles can exceed £90k-£120k+ with bonuses and travel allowances.

qualifications: Experience in CNC machining, programming, or service engineering
Strong knowledge of CAM software, tooling, and machining strategies
Ability to analyse machining performance and optimise processes
Understanding of materials, tolerances, and advanced manufacturing

lifestyle.description: CNC applications engineers work for OEMs, tooling companies, and advanced manufacturing firms. They support customers with process optimisation, training, and technical expertise. The role suits individuals who enjoy problem‑solving, customer interaction, and pushing machining performance to its limits.

habits: Advises customers on tooling, feeds, speeds, and strategies
Runs machining trials and performance tests
Collaborates with R&D and product teams to improve CNC systems
